---
description: "Use when designing, building, styling, reviewing, fixing, or refactoring UI, UX, frontend, renderer, HTML, CSS, layout, visual design, dashboards, overlays, HUDs, or interactive interface work. Requires both frontend-design and macmikey-claude-ui-ux-pro-max-skill to be loaded and followed unless the user explicitly overrides this behavior."
name: "UI Skill Requirements"
---
# UI Skill Requirements

- For any UI, UX, frontend, visual design, layout, styling, or interaction task, load both of these skills before planning or coding:
- `frontend-design`
- `macmikey-claude-ui-ux-pro-max-skill`
- Follow the thinking, design strategy, and implementation guidance from both skills for the duration of the task.
- Treat these as mandatory requirements, not optional references, unless the user explicitly instructs otherwise.
- When the skills overlap, combine them conservatively: keep the design direction intentional and distinctive, while still meeting accessibility, responsiveness, and interaction-quality guidance.
- If a task is only partly UI-related, apply this instruction to the UI-facing portion of the work and keep unrelated changes minimal.
